name: BudgetOperationContracts
description: Concrete versioned request, result, authority, idempotency, limit, and stable error contracts for the five public BUDGET operations plus shared contract discovery.
schema_def: All operations use the common Tally envelope, source-generated JSON, unknown-field rejection, canonical Money strings, DateOnly period boundaries, opaque ULIDs, one stdout business result, and metadata-only stderr. The required common envelope actor supplies attribution; actor is not duplicated inside operation input. Trusted OS owner/delegated authority is captured by the host context, not accepted as caller JSON.

budget.plan.draft.create input {period:{year,month,currencyCode:ZAR}, entries:[{categoryId,plannedAmount}], reason}; mutation key required; result BudgetPlanRevisionMutationResult containing immutable revision payload and exact DraftCreated event snapshot, with no live period/category/status enrichment.
budget.plan.revision.get input {revisionId}; result BudgetPlanRevisionDetail containing immutable revision payload plus current supplemental CategoryLifecycleEvidence.

budget.plan.revision.list input {period:{year,month,currencyCode:ZAR}}; result {periodState, planState, planId?, activeRevisionId?, revisions:BudgetPlanRevisionSummary[]} ordered createdAt then revisionId; summary contains identity, number, status, actor/reason, creation and activation/supersession provenance, but no entries or live category evidence.

budget.plan.revision.activate input {revisionId, reason}; trusted owner/delegated host context and mutation key required; result BudgetPlanRevisionMutationResult containing immutable payload plus exact activation and prior-supersession event snapshots, not later live status.

budget.position.get input {period:{year,month,currencyCode:ZAR}, revisionId?}; selected revision must belong to the supplied period/currency or BUDGET-REVISION-PERIOD-MISMATCH is returned before LEDGER; result BudgetPosition or explicit NoActiveBudgetPlanRevision.
system.schema.list/show discover descriptors without owner data.

Stable module errors include validation, revision-period-mismatch, period-closed, plan/revision-not-found, no-active-revision, category-invalid/inactive/stale, authority-required, lifecycle-invalid, idempotency-conflict, busy, LEDGER-incompatible/unavailable/snapshot-invalid, integrity, host-resource, and sanitized internal categories mapped to shared exit classes. There is no status, Save, edit, delete, copy, rollover, reopen, or rebalance operation.
